steps.title: Understand what we're finding
steps.content: The solution to a system of two linear equations is the point $(x, y)$ where the graphs of both equations intersect on the coordinate plane.

steps.title: Read the intersection point from the graph
steps.content:
Looking at the graph, the two lines intersect at the point $(-2, 2)$. The $y$-coordinate of this intersection is 2.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
